user_query:
I run a neighborhood coffee shop and want to turn my draft promos into a localized content kit and a simple 4-week calendar we can reuse. Please use these inputs: input/messages.csv, input/performance.csv, and input/jargon.txt. Create all outputs under output/.

Do the following:
1) Rewrite the messages for clarity and brevity while keeping them locally relevant.
- Keep the placeholder {BusinessName} unchanged in every rewrite.
- Ensure the neighborhood name from the input row appears verbatim in the rewrite.
- Remove any term listed in input/jargon.txt from the subject/body; do not replace with other jargon.
- For SMS rows: produce a rewritten_message of 160 characters or fewer; include a single "CTA:" segment near the end with a concrete action (e.g., "CTA: Visit today"), and keep {BusinessName} and the neighborhood name.
- For Email rows: produce a rewritten_subject (<=60 characters, includes the neighborhood, no jargon) and a rewritten_message body (<=120 words, includes the neighborhood, contains exactly one line starting with "CTA:"), and keep {BusinessName} unchanged.

2) Compute basic statistics and aggregates based on input/performance.csv and message lengths.
- For each row in input/performance.csv, compute open_rate = opened/sent and click_rate = clicked/sent (round to 4 decimals in outputs).
- Aggregate by channel: average open_rate and average click_rate across neighborhoods; also compute original_avg_char_count by channel from input/messages.csv (character count of the message field) and rewritten_avg_char_count by channel from your rewritten outputs (character count of rewritten_message).
- Aggregate by neighborhood: average open_rate and average click_rate across channels; determine top_channel_by_click_rate per neighborhood by selecting the channel with the higher click_rate in that neighborhood (break ties by choosing Email).

3) Write the following output files:
- output/rewritten_messages.csv with columns: id, neighborhood, channel, original_message, rewritten_subject, rewritten_message, rewritten_char_count, rewritten_word_count, contains_business_name_placeholder (true/false), contains_neighborhood_name (true/false), jargon_terms_removed (semicolon-separated), cta_present (true/false). For SMS, rewritten_subject should be empty; for Email, populate it.
- output/stats_by_channel.csv with columns: channel, avg_open_rate, avg_click_rate, original_avg_char_count, rewritten_avg_char_count.
- output/stats_by_neighborhood.csv with columns: neighborhood, avg_open_rate, avg_click_rate, top_channel_by_click_rate.
- output/content_calendar.csv with columns: week_number (1-4), neighborhood, channel, message_id, subject_if_email, scheduled_day_of_week. Use the top_channel_by_click_rate per neighborhood. Pick a rewritten message for that neighborhood+channel; if multiple exist, rotate them in order. For scheduled_day_of_week, use Monday for all rows. For Email rows, fill subject_if_email with the rewritten_subject; for SMS, leave subject_if_email empty.

Constraints:
- Use only the provided input files; do not perform any network calls.
- Do not drop any messages; produce a rewritten row for each input row.
- Ensure every rewritten row mentions the exact neighborhood and keeps {BusinessName} unchanged.
- Ensure no term from input/jargon.txt appears in any rewritten_subject or rewritten_message.
- Rates must be rounded to 4 decimals in the stats outputs.

Inputs are small; please keep everything deterministic and write all files to the specified output paths so I can review and spot-check. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
